user_query:
I’m organizing our June newsletter and an outreach email for the Okaloosa Dems and need your help coordinating content from the provided local files. Please complete all three parts and save the outputs under the output/ folder as specified.

Inputs (in workspace):
- input/saved_webpage.html (a static snapshot with a table of key 2024 primary dates for Okaloosa County)
- input/meeting_notes.txt (two dated local items for our club)
- input/rough_letter.md (a messy draft welcome note for the June newsletter)

Required outputs:
1) Extract structured dates into output/key_dates.csv
   - Parse every dated item in input/saved_webpage.html and input/meeting_notes.txt.
   - Output CSV columns in this exact order: source, item_type, title, date_start, date_end, time, location, notes.
   - Fill source as "webpage" for items from the HTML table and "notes" for items from meeting_notes.txt.
   - Normalize dates to YYYY-MM-DD. If an item covers a range (e.g., Early Voting), set date_start and date_end accordingly; if it’s a single-day item, set date_end equal to date_start.
   - Set item_type using these categories: registration_deadline, early_voting, election_day, meeting, volunteering (choose the closest match for each item).
   - Include all items found in the two files (there should be five total rows) and preserve the exact event titles from the sources in the title field.

2) Rewrite the rough letter into output/updated_welcome_note.md
   - Use input/rough_letter.md as the base text. Remove placeholders, fix grammar, tighten structure, and keep it friendly, inclusive, and community-focused for Okaloosa County. Avoid partisan slogans.
   - Length: 300–350 words.
   - Include a short bulleted list that names and dates these three items exactly as titled in output/key_dates.csv: "Book Closing for Primary (Voter Registration Deadline)", "Early Voting (Primary)", and "Primary Election - Election Day".
   - Mention both club items from meeting_notes.txt (the monthly meeting and the library tabling) with their dates and locations, using the normalized data from output/key_dates.csv as the single source of truth for titles and dates.

3) Draft a volunteer email into output/volunteer_email.txt
   - Start with a single line: Subject: <your subject here>
   - Then write a concise body (180–250 words) addressed to our volunteers in Okaloosa County. Informative, welcoming tone; avoid partisan slogans.
   - Include a brief list in the body that references at least three upcoming items drawn from output/key_dates.csv, explicitly including "Voter Registration Tabling" and "Okaloosa Dems Monthly Meeting" from the notes, plus at least one election-related item from the webpage.
   - Ask volunteers to RSVP for the Fort Walton Beach Library tabling by replying to the email. Use the titles and dates exactly as they appear in output/key_dates.csv.

General constraints:
- Do not invent dates or titles; use the extracted CSV as the single source of truth when writing the note and email.
- Keep all outputs self-contained and ready to send/publish.

Deliverables to produce:
- output/key_dates.csv
- output/updated_welcome_note.md
- output/volunteer_email.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
